mechanism: FIFO-driven margin overstatement provides the appearance of pricing power, which companies then protect through complementary non-GAAP adjustments. Both distortions compound to make reported earnings increasingly divorced from economic reality during commodity inflation.
regime_dependence: Both effects are mechanically linked to commodity price increases and reverse when prices decline. LIFO/FIFO is deterministic; non-GAAP widening involves managerial discretion and tends to persist longer (sticky adjustments).
